token_reduction.method: scripts/graph/measure_kanban_savings.sh + scripts/graph/measure_spec_context_savings.sh run from repo root with PATH=/tmp/kuzu-venv/bin:$PATH
token_reduction.note: Per-skill before/after byte counts are not produced by the two shipped measurement scripts — they emit family-level aggregates against canonical fixtures (tests/fixtures/kanban-500.json, tests/fixtures/specs-30/). The 22 refactored skills all cite either prompts/kanban-write-protocol.md or prompts/graph-read-protocol.md (grep confirms 25 files do so; the 22 figure in MEMORY.md excludes skills/index and skills/impact which are the graph layer itself plus the trace/stub-hunt/smoke-test/qa-ui-review/research/insights/doc-review/config-sync group that only cite the protocols for write-invalidation purposes). Both aggregate ratios are identical at 6.7x, meeting the 5x design target with a 1.7x margin.

cold_index.note: Measures code-graph portion only (File/Symbol/REFERENCES nodes) via a 108-line validation driver; does not include Task/Subtask/Spec/Feature/Module/_source families that the full cc-master:index writes. Passes the 60s scaling gate with 53s margin. Per subtask #115, cc-master:index is a Claude Code slash command and has no shell-invokable driver — any reproduction must construct one.

touch_reindex.method: Single-file MERGE upsert proxy measured via embedded Python at /tmp/touch-proxy2.py — kuzu_client.py has no --touch subcommand (confirmed via --help: only init/query/close). The --touch <file> flag belongs to the cc-master:index skill, which is a slash command and cannot be driven non-interactively. Proxy timed 3 runs of: read .cc-master/kanban.json -> open DB -> MERGE (:File {path}) -> close. This models the minimum real work --touch would perform per file.
touch_reindex.note: Proxy is lower-bound (single MERGE, no secondary Task/Subtask/BLOCKED_BY rewrites that the real --touch .cc-master/kanban.json would perform). Even so it is 5x under the 0.2s target. True end-to-end --touch measurement requires a shell-invokable driver for cc-master:index, which is not shipped in v0.21.0 — subtask #117 should flag this as a known gap in the release doc and recommend it as a v0.22 instrumentation task. Subtask #115's 6.9s 'warm re-run' number was a full-walk re-index, not a single-file touch, and should NOT be quoted as the --touch latency.

intra_file.note: 3 definitions + 9 distinct call sites across scripts/graph/kuzu_client.py and its two worktree copies; all recovered by graph.

cross_file.note: Global cross-file REFERENCES count in the entire graph is 0 out of 306 total edges (`MATCH (f:File)-[r:REFERENCES]->(s:Symbol) WHERE f.path <> s.file RETURN count(*)` returned 0). This is the ast-grep v1 dynamic-dispatch gap documented in MEMORY.md: the walker matches call-site symbols textually but does not resolve cross-module imports to the defining Symbol node. SCIP swap in v0.22 (per docs/plans/2026-04-graph-engine-v1.md) will close this gap. Precision is still 100% — every graph hit is a real call — but recall collapses on symbols whose primary usage is in a different file than the definition. Impact skill output therefore SHOULD document this limitation in the v0.21.0 release notes (subtask #117).

impact_accuracy.overall_note: The MEMORY.md claim of '~80% accuracy' for ast-grep v1 holds for combined precision across both tests (24/24 = 100% precision) but overall recall on cross-file symbols is near zero. The ~80% figure appears to be an upper-bound estimate on a codebase where most references are intra-file; cc-master itself has heavier cross-file coupling via Python imports, so recall here is worse.

graceful_degradation.exit_code_source: subtask #115 Operation 4: moved .cc-master/graph.kuzu aside, ran kuzu_client.py query, captured exit=3 and JSON error {"error": "database not found at ..."}
indicator_strings_found: Graph: stale — fell back to JSON
Graph: absent — fell back to JSON
graceful_degradation.note: Verified by Grep against the worktree SKILL.md. First-run check (line 38), Step 6 header 'Emit Graph Output Indicator' (line 234), and all three literal indicator strings (lines 238-240) are present. The default-on-error clause at line 242 instructs skills to emit 'Graph: absent — fell back to JSON' if a pre-query check errored, closing the indicator contract. kanban skill is representative of the 22 refactored skills — task #20's indicator wiring is confirmed in-place.

regressions.design_target_source: docs/plans/2026-04-graph-engine-v1.md (MEMORY.md cites '~80% accuracy' for ast-grep v1)
regressions.planned_fix: SCIP swap in v0.22 replaces ast-grep walker while preserving the Kuzu schema; REFERENCES edge count is expected to rise dramatically with cross-module resolution.

Expose --touch as a kuzu_client.py subcommand OR ship a shell-invokable driver for cc-master:index so future validation runs can measure the real single-file re-index latency. MEMORY.md note '--touch fires unconditionally; cc-master:index handles graph-absent internally' is accurate but not shell-reachable in v0.21.0.
